Percentage of staff turnover
variation: Workforce turnover has returned to levels more consistent with previous years, following last year’s spike driven by sector-wide competition and internal restructuring.

description: Health inspections of aquatic facilities
variation: Council continues to take a proactive approach to the sampling of the two aquatic facilities within the municipality.

description: Utilisation of aquatic facilities
variation: Membership and WynSwim enrolment have increased over the past 12 months and driven higher levels of visitation. The rate of visitation growth has been marginally outpaced by Wyndham’s population growth, leading to a consistent indicator when compared to the previous 12 month period.

description: Cost of aquatic facilities
variation: Increased enrolments in the Leam to Swim program, as well as higher numbers of Health and Fitness members, has driven positive outcomes in relation to both financial performance and visitations.

description: Time taken to action animal management requests
variation: Time taken to action animal management requests are slightly higher than previous year, due to an increase in workload and resource limitations. Consistency by staff and appropriate procedures have mitigated the result.

description: Animals reclaimed
variation: Slight decrease in the number of animals reclaimed, largely due to a 14 percent increase in the number of  animals collected, when compared to the 2023/24 financial year. Ongoing economic constraints and financial hardship contributing to a continued rise in both surrendered and deliberately unclaimed impounded animals.

description: Animals rehomed
variation: Decrease of 12 per cent on the previous financial year is related to a 247 increase in the number of unclaimed collected animals, when compared to the  2023/24 financial year. Ongoing relationships with compliant community foster carer networks proved to be valuable in finding new homes for a substantial number of pets, as did animal business adoption events held by partnered organisations for the rehoming of eligible dogs and cats.

description: Cost of animal management service per population
variation: Process improvements have contributed to a 17 per cent decrease in the cost of running the Animal Management service.

description: Animal management prosecutions
variation: There were nine prosecutions for the financial year, which led to nine successful court outcomes.  Majority of the prosecutions were dog related.

description: Time taken to action food complaints
variation: The time taken to action food complaints has remained relatively unchanged as a result of improved business practices and procedures.

description: Food safety assessments
variation: Indicator is measured by calendar year and all inspections were completed for 2024.

description: Cost of food safety service
variation: A 10 per cent decrease in cost is primarily due to improved processes and an increased number of food premises.

description: Critical and major non-compliance outcome notifications
variation: All fourteen critical and major-non compliances referred to Council were actioned.

description: Food safety samples
variation: Food sampling obtained remains high for the 2024 calendar year. The result shows Councils continual commitment to food safety surveillance within the community.

description: Council decisions made at meetings closed to the public
variation: Result is slightly higher than the previous year. While there were less confidential resolutions considered for the year, there were also less total resolutions made for the year, noting the election period during September to October 2024.  Sixteen decisions were made at Council meetings while closed to the public. These confidential decisions are made to protect the personal and commercial interests of third parties.

description: Satisfaction with community consultation and engagement
variation: The Annual Community Satisfaction Survey was conducted from November 2024 until the end of January 2025, as a door-to-door-in-person style survey. The result increased by 3 percent on the prior year and consistent with last year’s Very Good rating. This equates to a 14 per cent increase on the forecast target.

description: Councillor attendance at council meetings
variation: Councillor in-person attendance at Council meetings is generally consistent and remains high. The option of on-line attendance when required also supports this result.

description: Cost of elected representation
variation: The increased cost of the Governance Service in 2024/25 is a result of the increase in councillor allowances, training costs associated with Councillor induction and administrative costs for onboarding of councillors following the 2024 Council election.

description: Satisfaction with council decisions
variation: The Annual Community Satisfaction Survey was conducted from November 2024 until the end of January 2025, as door-to-door-in-person style survey. The result increased by 3 percent and is consistent with last year's Good rating.

description: Recently purchased library collection
variation: The age of the collection has remained stable over the financial year as new acquisitions have kept pace with the removal of ageing stock.

description: Cost of library service per population
variation: Cost of the library service while slightly lower, remains consistent with prior year.

description: Loans per head of population
variation: Utilisation of the Library collection remains steady for the 2024/25 financial year.

description: Library membership
variation: Library registrations for the 2024/25 financial year increased by 8 per cent on the prior year and maybe a reflection of the Hoppers Crossing library re-opening.

description: Library visits per head of population
variation: Slight increase of 4 percent on library visits when compared to the 2024-25 financial year.

description: Infant enrolments in the MCH service
variation: Birth notices within the municipality remain high, reflecting sustained population growth and continued strong demand for Maternal and Child Health services.

description: Cost of the MCH service
variation: Improved efficiencies in the cost of providing the MCH service has enabled an expanded service delivery to occur over the 2024/25 financial year.

description: Participation in the MCH service
variation: Participation rates have shown continuous improvement over the past two years, increasing by 13 per cent on the prior financial year. This sustained growth, has been achieved despite ongoing workforce pressures and reflects the team’s strong focus and commitment to enhancing engagement with families.

description: Participation in the MCH service by Aboriginal children
variation: Engagement with Aboriginal families has continued to improve over the past two years, increasing by 11 per cent on the prior financial year. The favourable result highlights Councils ongoing commitment to providing culturally safe, accessible, and responsive services.

description: Participation in 4-week Key Age and Stage visit
variation: Participation rates increased slightly reflecting the service's ongoing commitment to early engagement with families.

description: Sealed local road requests
variation: Improved weather conditions, in comparison to the prior financial year and Council's pro-active road condition assessment program, has seen overall volumes of pothole requests decrease by 7 per cent on the prior year.

description: Sealed local roads maintained to condition standards
variation: Council continues to have a pro-active road condition assessment program resulting in appropriate action to ensure Council’s road standards are maintained. Result has remained stable over the years and is likely to continue. Result is 1 per cent better than the forecast target.

description: Cost of sealed local road reconstruction
variation: Lower material costs and increased competition from a slower market, driving tender prices down have both contributed to unit price improvements of 10 per cent.

description: Cost of sealed local road resealing
variation: Increased construction costs have contributed to a slightly higher cost involved in resealing sealed local roads.

description: Satisfaction with sealed local roads
variation: The Annual Community Satisfaction Survey was conducted from November 2024 until the end of January 2025, as door-to-door-in-person style survey. This indicator has increased from Good to Very Good rating this year. Through continued consultation with the community, Council understands the impact that traffic congestion and the construction of roads has on the municipality. Council remains committed to advocating on behalf of the community regarding matters related to the local transport network.

description: Time taken to decide planning applications
variation: Slight increase of 6 per cent in the median number of days when compared to the same period last year, is attributable to an 8 per cent increase in applications in the 2024/25 financial year. The median number of days takes into account all parts of the application process including submitting further information by the applicant and advertising. Council uses a collaborative approach when deciding on planning applications and in some instances this leads to unmet timeframes.

description: Planning applications decided within required time frames
variation: Although Council aims to meet the required statutory time frames, Council works closely with applicants to achieve the best outcomes for everyone involved. This collaborative approach as well as the complexity associated with some applications can contribute to additional delays. Result is 6.6 per cent over the annual target.

description: Cost of statutory planning service
variation: The cost of the Statutory Planning service has remained consistent when compared to the prior financial year.

description: Council planning decisions upheld at VCAT
variation: VCAT has affirmed Council’s decision on two occasions from the six appearances at the Tribunal this financial year.

description: Kerbside collection bins missed
variation: Continued monitoring and reviewing of missed bin requests has seen a 20 per cent improvement on the prior year results. The calculation reflects instances where the driver failed to collect the bin and does not include missed collections resulting from resident errors or bin access issues.

description: Cost of kerbside garbage bin collection service
variation: Despite an increase in the landfill levy, an incremental increase to the costs of delivering a waste collection service, and an increase of 5,839 of bins required to be collected, there is only a slight increase of 3 per cent on the prior year.

description: Cost of kerbside recyclables collection service
variation: Costs are 9 per cent lower than for the same period last financial year. This is due to a reduction in tonnes collected for recycling possibly due to the container deposit scheme which began in November 2023 as well as a new cost effective recycling processing contract which commenced in August 2024.

description: Kerbside collection waste diverted from landfill
variation: The slight decrease could be due to the introduction of the Victorian Container Deposit Scheme and a reduction in green waste yield. The average weight of green waste per household has decreased 13 per cent, primarily due to fewer bin collections and lower rainfall compared to the previous financial year. Result is 3 per cent below the forecast target.

description: Expenses per property assessment
variation:
Since 2021–22, the cost per property assessment has risen by $472. With efficiency measures, it is forecast to decrease $74 by 2028–29. 
In 2024–25,CPI added about $119 per assessment, but this was offset by total expenses that increased more slowly than the number of assessments.

description: Average rate per property assessment
variation: The result for this measure has increased by $162 per assessment since 2021-22 and it is expected to increase by a further $176 per assessment by 2028-29.

description: Current assets compared to current liabilities
variation: The result for this measure decreased by 26.02% compared to 2023/24. The movement from the previous year  is primarily due to growth in term deposits with maturities over 12 months.

description: Unrestricted cash compared to current liabilities
variation: The decline in the ratio for 2024/25 is attributed to cash holdings moved to term deposits with maturities over 12 months to take advantage of higher interest rates.

description: Loans and borrowings compared to rates
variation: The loans to rates ratio has decreased compared to prior year due to both the unchanged level of borrowings and  increased rates revenue in line with the rate cap. This indicator is forecast to rise in 2025/26 as a result of additional $20.7 million in borrowings budgeted in 2025/26.

description: Loans and borrowings repayments compared to rates
variation: This ratio is inline with 23/24 with no new borrowings during the year.

description: Non-current liabilities compared to own source revenue
variation: This ratio reduced in 2024/25 compared to last year mainly due to an increase in own source revenue driven by the early payment of the Financial Assistance Grants. Also, during 2024/25 there was a reduction of non current liabilities as Council's $15M borrowings were moved to current liabilities for repayment next year. The ratio is expected to increase for future years, as Council intends to secure additional borrowings to assist in the funding of the capital works program. Any borrowings undertaken will be subject to Council endorsement at that time.

description: Asset renewal and upgrade compared to depreciation
variation: The actuals for upgrade and renewal expenditure are lower than budget expectations due to lower than anticipated depreciation. The increase from the 2023/24 result is driven by increased upgrade expenditure across all asset classes.

description: Adjusted underlying surplus (or deficit)
variation: The ratio increased compared to the previous year due to the early payment of the 2025/26 Financial Assistance Grants. The impact of this early payment is compounded by the absence of any early payments during 2023/24. In addition, there has also been a decrease in materials and services spend from 2023/24 as the result of a reduction in recycling costs.

description: Rates compared to adjusted underlying revenue
variation: By the end of 2024/25, the proportion of revenue from various sources relative to rates revenue decreased, mainly because of the early payment of the 2025/26 Financial Assistance Grants and the absence of any early payments during 2023/24. This also has resulted in the variance to target.

description: Rates compared to property values
variation: Rates revenue compared to property values remains at a consistent level.

description: Expenses per head of municipal population
variation: The result for this measure has increased by $77 per capita over the 4 year period to $1,540 in 2025. This increase is in line with CPI increase and growth within the municipality.

description: Infrastructure per head of municipal population
variation: The result for this measure is in line with prior year result and indicates Council's strong commitment to improving infrastructure.

description: Population density per length of road
variation: The Population Density per Length of Road has increased by 2 per cent from 177.97 to 181 persons per kilometre of road. This marginal rise suggests a modest increase in the concentration of population relative to the available road infrastructure.

description: Own-source revenue per head of municipal population
variation: Compared to 2023/24, the Council generated a higher amount of its own revenue from sources such as interest income and rates that increased in-line with CPI.

description: Recurrent grants per head of municipal population
variation: The ratio increased compared to the previous year due to the early payment of the 2025/26 financial assistance grant. The impact of this early payment is compounded by the absence of any early payments during 2023/24.

description: Relative Socio-Economic Disadvantage
variation: Based on the data from the 2021 Census, and released from the Australian Bureau of Statistics, the indicator suggests the economic and social conditions of people and households within Wyndham are placed in the mid-to-upper range, suggesting generally better access to resources, education, and employment compared to lower-decile areas.

description: Percentage of staff turnover
variation: Staff turnover rates have remained consistent when compared to the prior financial year.

description: Health inspections of aquatic facilities
variation: Annual inspection for each site at Richmond Recreation Centre, Collingwood Leisure Centre, Fitzroy Swimming Pool.

description: Utilisation of aquatic facilities
variation: Utilisation is slightly down due to the completion of capital works projects and maintenance at all sites resulting in temporary closures.

description: Cost of aquatic facilities
variation: Reduction in service delivery staff has influenced the reduction in cost of Aquatic facilities.

description: Time taken to action animal management requests
variation: Council continues to take all necessary steps to action animal management requests in a timely manner. Time taken to action animal management requests is under the target of three days.

description: Animals reclaimed
variation: Council collected 382 animals this year compared to 458 animals in 2023/24, a decrease of 17%. This year 181 animals were reclaimed by their owners compared with 186 in 2023/24.

description: Animals rehomed
variation: This figure has decreased from 2023/24. The figure does not include feral and semi-wild animals which are unable to be rehomed.

description: Cost of animal management service per population
variation: Decrease in cost of the animal management service which is consistent with previous years reporting.

description: Animal management prosecutions
variation: There were a total of eight animal management related prosecutions and all have been successful

description: Time taken to action food complaints
variation: The time taken to respond to food-related complaints increased in the second half of 2024, resulting in a higher overall response rate. Our efforts remain focused on ensuring timely and effective service for the community.

description: Food safety assessments
variation: Council maintained a high level of food safety assessments across registered premises throughout the year. This ensured continued protection of public health and safety for the community.

description: Cost of food safety service
variation: The cost of delivering the food safety service increased slightly over the past year, reflecting changes in the number of registered premises. However, the service continues to operate efficiently within the expected range and remains aligned with average rates across Councils, ensuring value for the community.

description: Critical and major non-compliance outcome notifications
variation: Council achieved a strong improvement in its follow-up rate for critical and major non-compliance notifications, reaching 98.82% in 2025 - an increase from 95.15% the previous year. This reflects Council’s ongoing commitment to ensuring food safety standards are upheld and that timely action is taken to protect public health.

description: Food safety samples
variation: Although fewer food samples were collected this year due to a delayed start to the program, Council continues to prioritise food safety and remains committed to rigorous monitoring practices that protect public health and maintain community confidence.

description: Council decisions made at meetings closed to the public
variation: Council has a commitment to ensuring transparent decision-making which includes a rigorous assessment of each proposed confidential report against the definition of confidential information in the Local Government Act.

description: Satisfaction with community consultation and engagement
variation: Council scored an average of 50% for Satisfaction with Community Consultation and Engagement in the Annual Customer Satisfaction Survey 2025. The community engagement program was impacted by the election caretaker period which resulted in engagements being postponed. This may have affected satisfaction ratings. Council is investing significantly in technology over the next two years to improve both internal processes and community outcomes.

description: Councillor attendance at council meetings
variation: The answer scale for the ACSS was updated in 2022 to align with the scale used in the Victorian Local Government Community Satisfaction Survey conducted by the Department of Government Services. This means that results prior to 2022 are not directly comparable with current results.

description: Cost of elected representation
variation: Attendance at Council meetings remains above 90% in line with prior years.

description: Satisfaction with council decisions
variation: The cost increase reflects training costs in line with the mandatory training required by the Local Government Act

description: Recently purchased library collection
variation: The library's collection continues to balance new acquisitions with the library's existing collection, and is maintained through regular reviews of borrowing data in line with contemporary collection management practices.

description: Cost of library service per population
variation: Service offerings continue to be highly utilised with high levels of community satisfaction despite the slight reduction in expenditure. Costs per head are reasonably consistent with other LGAs who provide high levels of service.

description: Loans per head of population
variation: Borrowing rates exceed national benchmarks.

description: Library membership
variation: Library membership exceeds national benchmarks. An analysis of our members’ postcodes shows a significant portion reside in neighbouring municipalities, and it’s believed that the proximity of our branches to public transport and positioning along major roads in/out of Yarra, is a contributing factor as to why so many non-residents use our service. Other initiatives such as the Open Library program (extended hours) and a high volume of programs and events, contribute to our high patronage.

description: Library visits per head of population
variation: Visits exceed national benchmarks.

description: Infant enrolments in the MCH service
variation: Infants are enrolled in the Maternal and Child Health Service by Council as part of the birth notification process. A result of >100% can occur where the birth and first home visit occur in different financial years.

description: Cost of the MCH service
variation: The cost of the service is based on overall cost divided by hours worked by nurses. There was a 5.47% increase in the birth numbers from the previous financial year but a reduction in cost of the service. This is mostly due to staffing vacancies during the year..

description: Participation in the MCH service
variation: Participation rates continue to be at a similar level to previous years. Nurses proactively follow up with families who do not attend scheduled appointments and provide flexible options to help maximise attendance.

description: Participation in the MCH service by Aboriginal children
variation: Participation rates continue at a similar trend from last financial year following data cleansing with the Victorian Aboriginal Health Service who were previously accessing Yarra’s Child Development Information System (CDIS).

description: Participation in 4-week Key Age and Stage visit
variation: Community participation in 4-week Key Age and Stage visits continues to be comparable with previous years results.

description: Sealed local road requests
variation: There has been a 9% increase in sealed local road requests since last year due to a general increase in community engagement and interest with council services

description: Sealed local roads maintained to condition standards
variation: Estimated based on latest condition assessment and capital funding since this condition assessment. Council plans on undertaking a comprehensive condition assessment of its Roads in the 25/26 financial year.

description: Cost of sealed local road reconstruction
variation: Council's local road reconstruction relates to bluestone pavements and as such can be quite complex in addition to normal cost escalation.

description: Cost of sealed local road resealing
variation: There has been as 11% increase in the cost of sealed local road reconstruction since last year. This is due to increases in cost and more complex projects in the 24/25 financial year

description: Satisfaction with sealed local roads
variation: Council scored an average of 59% for Satisfaction with sealed local roads in the Annual Customer Satisfaction Survey (ACSS) is consistent with last years result.

description: Time taken to decide planning applications
variation: The time taken to decide planning applications has improved by approximately 9.1% over the reporting period. This is attributed to the decrease of applications, which is consistent across the industry.

description: Planning applications decided within required time frames
variation: The number of planning applications decided within the statutory timeframe has improved over the reporting period. This is attributed to the decrease of applications, which is consistent  across the industry.

description: Cost of statutory planning service
variation: This is an increase of approximately 2.85% compared to the previous year and is attributed to the general increase in wages and external peer review services required.

description: Council planning decisions upheld at VCAT
variation: Yarra continues to have a significant number of appeals related to complex and controversial applications however the number of appeals to VCAT has been decreasing over the last few financial years with 65 appeals lodged in 2024/25 compared to 76 appeals lodged in 2023/24 and 79 appeals lodged in 2022/23. The number of Council decisions upheld at VCAT is 70% in 2024/25. Significantly, 43% of refusal appeals and 57% of failure appeals were settled via the consent of the parties. In 2024/25 there were a total of 42 decisions (68%) which were settled via the consent of the parties.

description: Kerbside collection bins missed
variation: Missed collections have increased, only change to kerbside services in 24/25 was the introduction of a fourth kerbside bin for the FOGO service.

description: Cost of kerbside garbage bin collection service
variation: Cost of garbage service has decreased due to the introduction of kerbside FOGO.

description: Cost of kerbside recyclables collection service
variation: Cost of recyclables are consistent, slightly less in tonnage however increase in processing costs.

description: Kerbside collection waste diverted from landfill
variation: Diversion rates have improved considerably mainly due to the introduction of councils kerbside FOGO collection service.

description: Expenses per property assessment
variation: Expenses per property assessment remain steady and on trend, these tracking this low in an inflationary environment is a testament to councils dedication to delivering value for money.

description: Average rate per property assessment
variation: The average rate per property is likely to grow in line with the forecast annual rate cap.

description: Current assets compared to current liabilities
variation: Improvement in indicator reflective of improved operating performance in the net result 30 June 2025 in line with Council's adopted Financial Sustainability Strategy, in addition to the early financial assistance payment received from the VLCGC.

description: Unrestricted cash compared to current liabilities
variation: There are increased funds held in term deposits compared to 2024, in addition to the early financial assistance payment received from the VLCGC.

description: Loans and borrowings compared to rates
variation: The improvement in the ratio is reflective of Councils commitment to reduce borrowings as per the Financial Sustainability Strategy.

description: Loans and borrowings repayments compared to rates
variation: The improvement in the ratio is reflective of Councils commitment to reduce borrowings as per the Financial Sustainability Strategy.

description: Non-current liabilities compared to own source revenue
variation: This indicator has reduced in large part due to the reduction of non-current loans and continued increases in parking revenues.

description: Asset renewal and upgrade compared to depreciation
variation: In line with the Financial Sustainability Strategy, Council aims for a renewal ratio of 100%, indicating assets are being replaced at the same rate as they are consumed. The 2025 improvement was driven by increased upgrades.

description: Adjusted underlying surplus (or deficit)
variation:
In line with the Financial Sustainability Strategy, Council aims for a renewal ratio of 100%, indicating assets are being replaced at the same rate as they are
consumed. The 2025 improvement was driven by increased upgrades.

description: Rates compared to adjusted underlying revenue
variation: The indicator has remained at a very similar level to FY2024 and is expected to remain steady.

description: Rates compared to property values
variation: Indicator is consistent with prior years and is expected to remain the same.

description: Expenses per head of municipal population
variation: The expense per head of population increasedby 1.74% compared to 2023/24, consistant with prior years.

description: Infrastructure per head of municipal population
variation: Indicator is consistent with prior years.

description: Population density per length of road
variation: Indicator is consistent with prior years.

description: Own-source revenue per head of municipal population
variation: Own source revenue remains consistent with 2023/24, only small increase given increase in population.

description: Recurrent grants per head of municipal population
variation: Increase in grants per population due to prepayment of VGCL grant during 2024/25

description: Relative Socio-Economic Disadvantage
variation: On average, Yarra has lower levels of disadvantage across the municipality with high levels of disadvantage concentrated in the main public housing areas within Richmond, Collingwood and Fitzroy. No change in decile from last financial year.

description: Percentage of staff turnover
variation: Slight decrease from last financial year. While turnover increased following the easing of COVID-19 restrictions, the current rate has decreased.

description: Health inspections of aquatic facilities
variation: All aquatic facilities were inspected by an authorised officer during the 2024-25 year to ensure safety and wellbeing of all patrons.

description: Utilisation of aquatic facilities
variation:
Overall visitation slightly declined.
The reporting period included an eight-week closure of the Yarra Centre to complete essential pool filtration and liner upgrade works. Noting, visits to outdoor splash parks are not included in this data.

description: Cost of aquatic facilities
variation: Costs were slightly lower during 2024-25 due to minor facility disruptions.

description: Time taken to action animal management requests
variation: Increase in the time taken to respond to requests is attributed to reflecting reporting requirements being rounded to one-day minimum when compared to the prior year. Additionally, an increase in the onboarding of new staff has initially impacted response times due to training and integration.

description: Animals reclaimed
variation: There was a modest rise in the total number of animals collected, with the ratio of cats to dogs aligning closely with the previous year's figures. Cats frequently fall into the semi-owned or unowned category, which often results in lower reclaim and reunification rates. In contrast, dogs continue to show consistently high reunification outcomes, as they are more commonly identified and successfully returned to their owners.

description: Animals rehomed
variation: Despite a moderate rise in overall animal collections, rehoming rates remained relatively consistent with figures from the previous financial year.

description: Cost of animal management service per population
variation: With a focus on service efficiency, process improvements and retention of staff, the delivery of the service has remained stable and within budget. Associated costs are similar to previous years and comparable with similar sized councils.

description: Animal management prosecutions
variation: Consistent with prior year. Reporting reflects Council’s ongoing commitment to thoroughly investigating community concerns.

description: Time taken to action food complaints
variation: Through process and system enhancements, our data collection and analysis allowed for a greater distinction between premises and complaint types compared to previous years. As a result, this number is reflective of food related complaints only. Planned system enhancements and implementations will reduce our response times for handling of complaints

description: Food safety assessments
variation: Retention of experienced staff and additional temporary resources have seen an increase in food safety assessments completed this financial year.

description: Cost of food safety service
variation: System and process improvements have enabled more accurate identification of food-related premises, refining the total count used in this measure. While Council continues to deliver the food safety service at a lower cost than comparable councils and within budget, the number of registered food premises declined by a greater proportion than the reduction in service costs. This has resulted in an increased cost per premises

description: Critical and major non-compliance outcome notifications
variation:
Decrease from prior year.
Fewer notifications were received by Council overall; this resulted in less follow-ups - and a lower score. However, Council continues to prioritise all major and critical non-compliance to protect community health.

description: Food safety samples
variation: Council maintains a consistent level of food sampling.

description: Council decisions made at meetings closed to the public
variation: Council continues to pride itself on open and transparent decision making. Only four of 99 council decisions were made at meetings closed to the public.

description: Satisfaction with community consultation and engagement
variation:
Slight increase to previous year.
Council scored 46 in the 2025 Community Satisfaction Survey for Consultation and Engagement. Residents from the Urban region reported the highest satisfaction score (50) and women as a population group reported the highest satisfaction (49).

description: Councillor attendance at council meetings
variation: Councillor attendance has improved over previous years. Having the options to join online in extenuating circumstances has assisted.

description: Cost of elected representation
variation: There was a small increase in cost of elected representation. Note: the Victorian Independent Remuneration Tribunal increases Mayor/Deputy Mayor/Councillor allowances each year.

description: Satisfaction with council decisions
variation: Council scored 46 in the 2025 Community Satisfaction Survey for this indicator, a notable increase from the previous year. Results were varied amongst different sub-groups of the community. Residents from the Urban region reported higher satisfaction (50) while residents from the Yarra Valley region reported the lowest satisfaction (38).

description: Recently purchased library collection
variation: Council expanded reporting on this indicator to include digital items available through Boo Book Consortium. There are over 35,000 more collection items compared to the previous year. Our target is to have more than 80 per cent of items less than five years. Some older books that are highly valued by the community are being retained for much longer than five years.

description: Cost of library service per population
variation: Although higher than previous years, the cost remains low. Being a semi-regional library service, costs are typically lower than most urban library services.

description: Loans per head of population
variation: The overall number of loans increased slightly during 2024-25. However, the growth in the municipal population outpaced this increase, resulting in a slight decline in the per capita borrowing rate. Overall, borrowing trends remain strong, with digital items continuing to be popular among borrowers.

description: Library membership
variation:
Increase from prior year.
Throughout the reporting period the service has focused on converting frequent visitors into members. This has resulted in growth of registrations. This approach has also been boosted through people reporting they find library services a valuable support when experiencing cost of living pressures.

description: Library visits per head of population
variation: Increase from prior year. Throughout the reporting period, the service has focused on converting frequent visitors into members. This has resulted in growth of registrations. This approach has also been boosted through people reporting they find library services a valuable support when experiencing cost-of-living pressures.

description: Infant enrolments in the MCH service
variation: The rate of enrolment in the Maternal and Child Health service by families with newborns continues to be very high, however fewer birth notifications were received during 2024-25 resulting in fewer total enrolments. Every birth notice received by Council can engage in the MCH Service. Council continues to provide services to residents from neighbouring councils, which has caused our participation rate to be slightly higher than 100 per cent.

description: Cost of the MCH service
variation: Costing remains stable. Increase in cost reflecting increase in payrates associated with EA change October 2024.

description: Participation in the MCH service
variation: Participation in Council's MCH service has remained consistent with previous years.

description: Participation in the MCH service by Aboriginal children
variation:
Participation in Council's MCH services by Aboriginal and Torres Straight children has remained consistent with previous levels. 

MCH staff continue to work with external partners to improve participation in MCH services by Aboriginal and Torres Straight Island children and embed cultural safety into practice.

description: Participation in 4-week Key Age and Stage visit
variation: The number of four-week Key Age and Stage visits was lower than birth notices received due to readmission to hospital for either mother or baby and temporarily staying in other local government areas. This is particularly relevant during the height of summer and extreme fire rating days

description: Sealed local road requests
variation: Council received a reduced number of sealed local road requests during 2024-25, which could be contributed to increased hot spot programming for drainage and continued effort in reducing response times.

description: Sealed local roads maintained to condition standards
variation: The reported figure remains unchanged from the previous year, as Council's sealed road condition audit is conducted on a three-year cycle.

description: Cost of sealed local road reconstruction
variation: This year, Council has completed three road rehabilitation projects: two rural spray seal local road sections and one rural collector road with asphalt seal.

description: Cost of sealed local road resealing
variation: Costs of road resealing have increased from previous years. In 2024-25, 56 roads have been resealed across the network.

description: Satisfaction with sealed local roads
variation: The Local Government Community Satisfaction Survey reported that satisfaction with sealed local roads increased from 37 to 41 this year. Higher scores were reported by residents from the Urban region, whereas the Hills, Yarra Valley and Healesville-Yarra Glen regions reported lower scores.

description: Time taken to decide planning applications
variation: Council has been able to stabilise its planning workforce and retain planners. Significant investment in training and upskilling has improved planner knowledge, enabling faster and more efficient planner assessment, processing and delivery of decisions. One factor continuing to inflate the median days is a concerted effort to resolve older applications. Since June 2024, 84 applications older than 180 days have been determined. As older applications are resolved, the median number will continue to improve.

description: Planning applications decided within required time frames
variation:
Improved timeframes from previous year.
Reducing planning decisions within required timeframes has been a key area of focus. This has been added by application volumes remaining stable, alongside the implementation of the system improvements.
The vacancy rate has reduced from the previous financial year with greater success in filling roles and retaining staff.

description: Cost of statutory planning service
variation: The 2.2 per cent increase in costs from the previous year is minimal. The number of incoming planning applications remains consistent with last year, and the number of decisions made is only slightly lower. The primary cost driver is staffing. The workforce numbers have remained stable, with lower turnover and improved staff retention. There has also been a consistent workload year on year. The improved decision turnaround times, as shown in SP1 and SP2, demonstrate that our current resources are delivering enhanced service output.

description: Council planning decisions upheld at VCAT
variation:
The 38 per cent overturn (10 of 26 decisions) were mainly refusal decisions, where VCAT decided to issue an approval. This proportion is quite high, suggesting an opportunity to ensure refusal decisions are strongly aligned to planning policy.
Consents and varied outcomes (39 per cent) reflect positive engagement and flexibility in decision making. The data suggests that the planning appeals process is functioning as a corrective mechanism, enabling outcomes that balance the use and development with community benefit.
The diversity of overturned cases highlights the importance of clear policy interpretation and ongoing dialogue between council, applicants, objectors and the tribunal.

description: Kerbside collection bins missed
variation: Improvement to previous year. 2023-24 figure included three months of weekly garbage collections whereas 2024-25 only included fortnight garbage collections.

description: Cost of kerbside garbage bin collection service
variation: The costs of the kerbside garbage bin collection service has fallen with fortnightly services. The increase in bins is due to some residents requesting a second bin. An error was identified with the 2023-24 figure, which incorrectly included GST in the total cost figure.

description: Cost of kerbside recyclables collection service
variation: Costs have fallen from previous year. This is partly due to an error with the 2023-24 figure, which incorrectly included GST.

description: Kerbside collection waste diverted from landfill
variation: Significant improvement reported. Council has continued to improve waste diversion from landfill. This ongoing progress reflects the successful adoption of the weekly Food Organics and Garden Organics (FOGO) bin collection service.

description: Expenses per property assessment
variation: The increase is reflective of the rising costs of materials and services incurred to maintain service levels. Further impacted by the storm clean-up costs incurred and Preparing Australian Communities expenditure related to the grant received.

description: Average rate per property assessment
variation: Increase is reflective of the rate cap increase, movement in property valuation and increase in the number of rateable assessments.

description: Current assets compared to current liabilities
variation: Stronger result has been reported due to the expected grant funding being received in advance.

description: Unrestricted cash compared to current liabilities
variation: Stronger result has been reported due to the Financial Assistance grant funding being received in advance and maturation of term deposits.

description: Loans and borrowings compared to rates
variation: Significant increase due to accessing budgeted borrowings in the financial year for the purpose of investment in the Capital expenditure program.

description: Loans and borrowings repayments compared to rates
variation: The stable trend is reflective of one repayment in financial year in relation to the budgeted loans being drawn down.

description: Non-current liabilities compared to own source revenue
variation: The movement in the result is driven by accessing budgeted borrowings impacting the non-current liabilities balance.

description: Asset renewal and upgrade compared to depreciation
variation: Increase in depreciation expense due to the completion of projects and subsequent capitalisation of the asset.

description: Adjusted underlying surplus (or deficit)
variation: The improved result is due to the timing of grants received.

description: Rates compared to adjusted underlying revenue
variation: The stable trend is reflective of rate revenue increasing from the previous year along with  total revenue which has been buoyed by the advance receipt grant funding.

description: Rates compared to property values
variation: With minimal growth in rateable properties and movements in valuation there is a stable trend being experienced.

description: Expenses per head of municipal population
variation: Slight increase from prior year due to increased expenditure in supporting communities recovering from natural disasters and further compounded by increases in materials to deliver services.

description: Infrastructure per head of municipal population
variation: Increase due to changes in property, plant as well as asset revaluations.

description: Population density per length of road
variation: Slight increase due to infill development and lot size.

description: Own-source revenue per head of municipal population
variation: Slight increase reflective of rate cap levied and growth in rateable properties.

description: Recurrent grants per head of municipal population
variation: The significant increase is due to the timing of the Financial Assistance Grants received with 50% of the 2025-2026 financial assistance grant received in 2024-2025 therefore two portions of funding received in the financial year (there is no substantial additional funding received).

description: Relative Socio-Economic Disadvantage
variation: Consistent with prior year

description: Percentage of staff turnover
variation: There is an overall decreasing trend in turnover of staff which can be linked to strong staff culture survey results.

description: Health inspections of aquatic facilities
variation: No inspections were conducted during the 2024/25 year.

description: Utilisation of aquatic facilities
variation: An increase in patronage is a result of seasonal/warmer weather conditions this summer period which has resulted in more visitors to the pool.

description: Cost of aquatic facilities
variation: The cost change is a result of increased day-to-day operational costs, particularly casual lifeguard wages.

description: Time taken to action animal management requests
variation: Council employs two Rangers. The response time is quicker as there is staff that look after the Northern section and another for the Southern section of the municipality.

description: Animals reclaimed
variation: The number of animals reclaimed for the 2024/25 FY has significantly increased from 2023-24 FY due to an increase in microchipping and animal registration rates, making identification and reuniting owners with their missing pets easier.

description: Animals rehomed
variation: The number of animals rehomed and reclaimed for the 2024/25 FY has significantly increased from 2023/24 FY probably due to a concerted effort by the Animal Management team to target areas within the Yarriambiack Shire that are known to have increased numbers of stray cats; where these cats have been getting fed by a few residents but aren’t taking ownership of them.

description: Cost of animal management service per population
Animal management prosecutions
variation: Council had 1 successful animal prosecution occur during the 2024/25 financial year.

description: Time taken to action food complaints
variation: An improvement in response time to complaints was seen during the 2024/25 reporting period due to increased staff availability.

description: Food safety assessments
Cost of food safety service
variation: Council's Environmental Health Officer went from .4EFT to .6EFT which increased the cost of the food safety service.

description: Critical and major non-compliance outcome notifications
variation: No non-compliance notifications issued in 2024/25.

description: Food safety samples
variation: Council’s food sampling program operates on a calendar year. Sample numbers are determined by the Department of Health and during both the 2024 and 2025 calendar years, 100% of required samples were collected.

description: Council decisions made at meetings closed to the public
Satisfaction with community consultation and engagement
variation: Based on the annual Community Satisfaction Survey completed in the 2024/25 financial year.

description: Councillor attendance at council meetings
variation: Councillor attendance was strong, with a total of five approved leaves of absence for four Councillors for meetings in 2024/25.

description: Cost of elected representation
variation: The cost increase is attributed to the 2024 Council Elections, which necessitated expenses for Councillor induction training in accordance with section 32 of the Local Government Act 2020 (the Act) and Mayoral training in accordance with section 27A. Additionally, the increase in travel expense claims, resulting from attendance at community meetings, events, and undertaking community consultations, has also contributed to this cost escalation.

description: Satisfaction with council decisions
variation: Based on the annual Community Satisfaction Survey completed in the 2024/25 financial year.

description: Recently purchased library collection
variation: In line with Expected Range. Council’s book purchasing program has remained unchanged resulting in stable figures over the previous 4 years.

description: Cost of library service per population
variation: Introduction to Self-Service Library hours allows for reduced operating costs with minimal impact on community’s access to libraries. Large increases in attendance at Library events and programs equates for further cost reduction per population.

description: Loans per head of population
variation: Council continues to assess member usage of titles and platforms to adapt our collection and bring usage into Expected Range.

description: Library membership
variation: Council continues to assess memberships and adapt services to encourage more patron use of the library. You do not need to be a member of our libraries to participate in activities and programs.

description: Library visits per head of population
variation: Large increase from 2023/24 year showing that Council is on track to increasing library visits in line with Expected Range.

description: Infant enrolments in the MCH service
Cost of the MCH service
variation: In April 2025 Maternal Child Health services transfered over to West Wimmera Health Services. As a result of the transfer, MCH staff were made redundant, which has led to the increase of the service costs.

description: Participation in the MCH service
Participation in the MCH service by Aboriginal children
Participation in 4-week Key Age and Stage visit
Sealed local road requests
variation: Sealed local road request are on the steady decline thanks the establishment of a new Flocon unit and high functioning Jetmaster units carrying out proactive maintenance on councils sealed network.

description: Sealed local roads maintained to condition standards
variation: Sealed road maintenance standard sitting at desired level.

description: Cost of sealed local road reconstruction
variation: Reconstruction square meter costs can vary due to nature and scope of the works. Drainage, traffic control and vegetation removal can have dramatic effects on overall cost, the past year has not experienced any additional items and projects were done very efficiently.

description: Cost of sealed local road resealing
variation: Increase it cost due to inflationary nature of imported bitumen.

description: Satisfaction with sealed local roads
variation: Satisfaction level remains low, heavily impacted by local misconception that council is responsible for the state arterial network.

description: Time taken to decide planning applications
variation: The statutory days provided by the regulations is 60 days, achieving 44 days continues to be within the acceptable number of days.

description: Planning applications decided within required time frames
variation: The decline of the number of applications determined outside the required time frame related to a higher number of applications with objections, and a higher number of applications where referral agencies requested further information or were delayed in responding.

description: Cost of statutory planning service
variation: The strategic planning portion of our planning functions has used a few consultants, which have been removed from total costs.

description: Council planning decisions upheld at VCAT
variation: No applications were determined by VCAT this year compared to Council having one successful planning decision upheld at VCAT for the 2023/24 FY.

description: Kerbside collection bins missed
variation: The 2024/25 result represents a single occurrence when several bins were missed in a glass collection. The bins were missed when an inexperienced driver was doing the collection. The situation was rectified.

description: Cost of kerbside garbage bin collection service
Cost of kerbside recyclables collection service
Kerbside collection waste diverted from landfill
Expenses per property assessment
variation: A detailed asset reconciliation and revaluation was undertaken in the 2024/25 financial year, where assets were found to be incorrectly recorded in the asset register resulting in an inflated asset value.  When the reconciliation and revaluation was processed, this resulted in assets or parts of assets being disposed off, therefore creating a loss on disposal.

description: Average rate per property assessment
Current assets compared to current liabilities
Unrestricted cash compared to current liabilities
variation:
Unrestricted cash reserves are decreasing as Council returns to a “normal” year where there has been more funding available to address covid, floods, drought etc.
If term deposits were included in the calculation of this ratio the result would be 110.28%.

description: Loans and borrowings compared to rates
Loans and borrowings repayments compared to rates
variation: Council drew down a loan at the end of the 3rd quarter in 2023/24.  This has resulted in an increase in  loans and borrowings while rates and charges have also increased.

description: Non-current liabilities compared to own source revenue
Asset renewal and upgrade compared to depreciation
variation: Is within the acceptable range.  Capital works program is starting to decrease compared to previous years with less grant money available for projects.  Depreciation has increase due to an error found in the assets data in the 2023/24 year.

description: Adjusted underlying surplus (or deficit)
variation: Is within the acceptable range.  Demonstrates the reliance of council on grants to undertake capital projects.  Movement is also a result of 50% of the Federal Assistance Grants being paid in the 2024/25 financial year.

description: Rates compared to adjusted underlying revenue
variation: Is within the acceptable range.  Demonstrates the reliance of council on grants to undertake capital projects.  Movement is also a result of 50% of the Federal Assistance Grants being paid in the 2024/25 financial year.

description: Rates compared to property values
variation: Is within the acceptable range.  Property values have been high over the previous years which has been driven by the market.  This spike is starting to level out and should remain constant in the coming years.

description: Expenses per head of municipal population
variation: Operation costs are continuing to increase while municipal population is remaining stable.  Council is actively reviewing service levels while trying to maintain community expectations.

description: Infrastructure per head of municipal population
variation: Council has been successful in obtaining grants to address infrastructure such as funding for the Hopetoun Swimming Pool Project, Hopetoun Recreation Reserve Netball Lights while municipal population has remained stable.

description: Population density per length of road
Own-source revenue per head of municipal population
variation: Council's own source revenue is limited due to the services that Council can afford to provide. The municipal population has remained stable.

description: Recurrent grants per head of municipal population
variation: Council received early payment of 50% of the 2025/26 Federal Assistance Grant from the Victorian Grants Commission.  This was paid in late June and has resulted in a significant increase in the recurrent grants while municipal population has remained stable.

description: Relative Socio-Economic Disadvantage
Percentage of staff turnover
variation:
The significant variance in our staff turnover is primarily due to the transfer of Maternal Child Health Services to West Wimmera Health Services, as well as the ongoing transfer of Kindergarten Centres to 'Emerge'.
Over the past 12 months, we have seen the exit of services in Hopetoun, Beulah, and Murtoa. Additionally, Warracknabeal, Rupanyup, and Minyip are scheduled to transfer during the 2025-2026 financial year. These transitions have substantially impacted our turnover rates.
